metadata.title: Checkbox
metadata.description: Give users a clear way to make selections, such as agreeing to terms or choosing multiple items from a list.

properties.description: A label used for users using assistive technologies like screen readers. When set, any children or label supplied will not be announced. This can also be used to display a control without a visual label, while still providing context to users using screen readers.
Whether the control is active by default.

Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
A unique identifier for the element.
Visual content to use as the control label.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
The value used in form data when the checkbox is checked.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Require a confirmation step"
  details="Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :require_a_confirmation_step, label: "Require a confirmation step", details: "Ensure all criteria are met before proceeding" %>

examples.name: Error state
examples.description: Checkbox with validation error message for required form fields.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="I agree to the terms"
  error="You must accept the terms to continue"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :i_agree_to_the_terms, label: "I agree to the terms", error: "You must accept the terms to continue" %>

examples.name: Help text
examples.description: Checkbox with descriptive details text to provide additional context about the option.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Send order notifications"
  details="You'll receive emails when orders are placed, fulfilled, or cancelled"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :send_order_notifications, label: "Send order notifications", details: "You'll receive emails when orders are placed, fulfilled, or cancelled" %>

examples.name: Disabled state
examples.description: Checkbox in disabled state with explanatory details about why it's unavailable.
examples.html_code:
<s-checkbox
  label="Advanced settings"
  disabled
  details="Contact your administrator to enable advanced settings"
></s-checkbox>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.check_box :advanced_settings, label: "Advanced settings", disabled: true, details: "Contact your administrator to enable advanced settings" %>

examples.name: Settings group
examples.description: Multiple checkboxes for different configuration options with helpful details.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-checkbox label="Show only published products" checked></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox
    label="Enable inventory tracking"
    details="Track inventory levels and receive low stock alerts"
    checked
  ></s-checkbox>
  <s-checkbox
    label="View customer data"
    details="Allow staff to access customer contact information and order history"
  ></s-checkbox>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.check_box :show_only_published_products, label: "Show only published products", checked: true %>
  <%= form.check_box :enable_inventory_tracking, label: "Enable inventory tracking", details: "Track inventory levels and receive low stock alerts", checked: true %>
  <%= form.check_box :view_customer_data, label: "View customer data", details: "Allow staff to access customer contact information and order history" %>
</s-stack>
